explanation: The user instruction 'turn around' means the robot should rotate 180 degrees. Since the robot can only rotate left or right, turning around requires two consecutive rotations to the right (180 degrees total). This is a standard interpretation of 'turn around' in navigation tasks. The state shows no objects are discussed or being held, so no object-related actions are needed. The plan consists of two Motion(RotateRight) actions to achieve the desired orientation.
